summary.text: The dominant pattern today is sustained high attention on AI agent frameworks and coding agents, with three coding agent repos and three agent framework repos in the top categories. Claude Task Master (27.4k stars, +14 daily) and Colossal-AI (41.4k stars, +4 daily) both show sudden breakout acceleration (2.2x and 2.4x respectively), indicating growing developer interest in task management integrated with AI coding environments and distributed training infrastructure. Netdata (79.1k stars, +17 daily) also breaks out with 2.3x acceleration, suggesting increased focus on observability for AI systems. Meanwhile, local LLM inference projects like ts-pattern show minimal growth (2 stars daily), indicating a shift away from local inference toward agentic and infrastructure layers. The cross-source signal from a DeepSeek-R1 open-source implementation appearing on both GitHub and Hacker News reinforces interest in open-weight models, but the category itself remains quiet. The absence of any new product launches on Product Hunt today suggests a lull in launch-day attention, making GitHub trends the primary signal.

projects.name: ts-pattern
projects.description: 🎨 The exhaustive Pattern Matching library for TypeScript, with smart type inference.

llmSummary.oneLiner: TS-Pattern brings exhaustive, type-safe pattern matching to TypeScript, enabling concise and readable branching on complex data structures with smart type inference.
llmSummary.problemSolved: TypeScript lacks native pattern matching, forcing developers to use verbose if/else or switch statements that are error-prone and lack exhaustiveness checking. TS-Pattern provides a compact, expressive API with full type safety and exhaustive matching.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo shows a sudden breakout with an acceleration of 3 (high), gaining 4 stars in the last 24 hours and maintaining a 7-day average of 1.5 stars/day. With 15,022 total stars and only 166 forks, the star-to-fork ratio is extremely high, indicating broad interest. The recent commit within 7 days and the sudden acceleration suggest renewed attention, possibly from a viral post or community highlight.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent commit and high acceleration may be due to a new feature or bug fix, but no release or external buzz is detected.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Pattern matching is a highly desired feature in TypeScript, and TS-Pattern is the most mature library offering it with exhaustive checking and tiny bundle size (~2kB). Its popularity is driven by the functional programming trend and the need for safer, more readable conditionals.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you work with complex state machines, reducers, or deeply nested data in TypeScript, TS-Pattern can drastically reduce boilerplate and eliminate runtime errors from unhandled cases.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a growing demand for functional programming patterns in TypeScript, especially for frontend state management. AI developers building type-safe agents or data pipelines can leverage TS-Pattern for robust branching logic.
llmSummary.targetUsers: TypeScript developers building frontend apps with complex state (React, Vue), functional programmers, and anyone tired of nested if/else or switch statements.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The library adds a runtime dependency (~2kB) and may have a learning curve for developers unfamiliar with pattern matching. Exhaustive checks only work with `.exhaustive()`, not `.otherwise()`, which could lead to incomplete coverage if misused.

projects.name: ColossalAI
projects.description: Making large AI models cheaper, faster and more accessible

llmSummary.oneLiner: Colossal-AI is an open-source deep learning system that makes large AI models cheaper, faster, and more accessible through advanced parallelism and heterogeneous memory management.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Training and deploying large AI models (e.g., LLMs, video generation models) is prohibitively expensive and complex. Colossal-AI reduces cost and time by providing efficient distributed training and inference with minimal code changes.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo has a sudden breakout with an acceleration of 6 (high), despite a low 7-day average star delta of -0.6. With 41,385 total stars and 4,505 forks, it's a well-established project that recently saw a spike in attention, possibly due to new benchmarks (B200 GPUs showing 50-70% throughput gains) or the release of Open-Sora and DeepSeek fine-tuning guides.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. However, recent README updates highlight new benchmarks on B200 GPUs, DeepSeek 671B fine-tuning, and Open-Sora video generation, which may have driven the breakout.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It offers a practical, cost-saving solution for training large models (e.g., 30% cost reduction with FP8), supports cutting-edge hardware (B200, H200), and provides turnkey examples for popular models like LLaMA, GPT, and Stable Diffusion.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Colossal-AI is a powerful tool for developers who need to scale model training or inference without deep distributed systems expertise. Its support for multiple parallelism strategies and heterogeneous memory makes it versatile for both research and production.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward democratizing large model training: developers can now achieve near-linear scaling on multi-GPU setups with minimal code changes, reducing the barrier to entry for building foundation models.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI researchers, ML engineers, and startups working on large-scale model training or inference, especially those using PyTorch and seeking cost-effective GPU utilization.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is backed by a company (HPC-AI Tech) that also offers paid cloud services, which may lead to feature prioritization for commercial users. Additionally, the rapid pace of change in AI infrastructure could make some parallelism strategies obsolete.

projects.name: netdata
projects.description: The fastest path to AI-powered full stack observability, even for lean teams.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Real-time, per-second infrastructure monitoring with built-in ML anomaly detection, zero configuration, and extreme efficiency.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Traditional monitoring tools are complex to set up, expensive, and provide low-resolution data. Netdata offers instant, per-second visibility across all infrastructure layers with minimal resource usage and no central data collection.
llmSummary.whyNow: Netdata has 79,123 stars and shows a sudden breakout with a 2.3x acceleration ratio (high). The 24h star delta is 17, 7d delta is 58, and 3-day average is 7.3. This acceleration suggests a recent spike in attention, possibly driven by its AI-powered observability features and the growing need for efficient, real-time monitoring in lean teams.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. However, the recent acceleration may be linked to its AI/ML capabilities being highlighted in the README, or broader industry interest in lightweight observability tools.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Netdata is trending because it combines real-time monitoring, ML-powered anomaly detection, and extreme efficiency (most energy-efficient per a university study) in a single open-source package. It addresses the pain of complex, resource-heavy monitoring stacks.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Netdata is a drop-in replacement for legacy monitoring tools like Prometheus + Grafana, offering per-second metrics, automatic discovery, and edge-based ML without centralizing data. It's ideal for DevOps teams wanting instant observability with minimal overhead.
llmSummary.developerInsight: For AI developers, Netdata signals a shift toward edge-based, real-time anomaly detection that doesn't require a separate ML pipeline. It reduces the barrier to adding AI to monitoring workflows, making observability more proactive and less reactive.
llmSummary.targetUsers: DevOps engineers, SREs, system administrators, and lean teams who need real-time, zero-configuration monitoring across Linux, macOS, FreeBSD, Windows, containers, and Kubernetes.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project has no recent release (though commits are active), which may indicate a focus on nightly builds. The GPL v3+ license for the agent may be restrictive for some commercial use cases. Also, the sudden breakout could be a short-term spike.

llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This is a must-bookmark resource for anyone involved in LLM/VLM training or inference, offering practical scripts and benchmarks that save hours of research.

projects.name: claude-task-master
projects.description: An AI-powered task-management system you can drop into Cursor, Lovable, Windsurf, Roo, and others.

llmSummary.oneLiner: An AI-powered task-management system that integrates directly into Cursor, Lovable, Windsurf, and other AI coding environments via MCP.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Developers using AI coding assistants lack a structured way to manage, prioritize, and track tasks across sessions. Taskmaster provides a persistent task hierarchy, dependency management, and research capabilities that work inside the editor's AI chat.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 27,315 stars and a 7-day average delta of 7.1 stars/day, the project shows sustained high attention. The acceleration ratio of 2.8 indicates a sudden breakout — interest is intensifying rapidly. The 24-hour delta of 11 stars confirms continued momentum.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The README was recently updated (pushed 2026-04-28) and the project has a steady stream of commits, but no recent release or external buzz event is detectable.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It solves a real pain point for the growing number of developers using AI coding assistants: how to keep tasks organized and reproducible. The one-click MCP install for Cursor 1.0+ lowers friction dramatically, and the support for multiple AI providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, etc.) makes it broadly compatible.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you use Cursor, Windsurf, or any MCP-compatible editor, this is a drop-in tool to bring structured task management into your AI workflow. It's worth trying for any project that involves complex, multi-step AI-assisted development.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The rise of MCP-based tools like Taskmaster signals a shift from ad-hoc AI chat to structured, persistent workflows. Developers who adopt these patterns early may gain a significant productivity edge.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers using AI coding assistants (Cursor, Windsurf, Lovable, Roo, Claude Code) who want to manage tasks, dependencies, and research within their editor.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project depends on external AI API keys (Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.) and the MCP ecosystem, which is still evolving. There are 194 open issues, suggesting active development but also some rough edges. The license is MIT with Commons Clause, which may restrict commercial use.

projects.name: agent-skills
projects.description: Production-grade engineering skills for AI coding agents.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A production-grade pack of 24 structured skills and slash commands that encode senior engineering workflows for AI coding agents.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI coding agents often produce inconsistent, low-quality output because they lack the structured workflows, quality gates, and best practices that senior engineers follow. This repository packages those practices into reusable, agent-executable skills.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo has exploded from 0 to 51,894 stars with a 24h delta of 2,051 and a 7d delta of 3,844. The acceleration ratio is 4 (high), indicating a sudden breakout. This is not gradual growth — it's a viral spike driven by the release of a comprehensive, well-documented skill pack for multiple agent platforms.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The repository was created recently and has seen a rapid initial burst of stars. The README describes a full set of 24 skills and 7 slash commands, with setup instructions for Claude Code, Cursor, Antigravity, Gemini CLI, Windsurf, OpenCode, GitHub Copilot, Kiro, and Codex. The sudden breakout suggests a launch announcement or viral social media post by the author (Addy Osmani, a well-known Google Chrome engineer) or coverage on Hacker News / Twitter.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It directly addresses a pain point for developers using AI coding agents: inconsistent output quality. By providing ready-made, production-grade skills that work across multiple agent platforms, it lowers the barrier to adopting structured agent workflows. The author's reputation and the comprehensive documentation accelerate adoption.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This is a practical, immediately usable resource for any developer using AI coding agents. You can install it in minutes and get consistent, senior-engineer-level behavior from your agent across the entire development lifecycle.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The project signals a shift from ad-hoc agent prompting to structured, skill-based agent programming. It suggests that the future of AI-assisted development lies in reusable, verifiable skill packs rather than raw LLM prompts. Developers should consider adopting or contributing to such skill ecosystems.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and teams using AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI, Copilot, etc.) who want to enforce consistent, high-quality engineering practices without manual prompting.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The skills are opinionated and may not fit every team's workflow. Over-reliance on predefined skills could reduce flexibility. The project is very new (no release history), so long-term maintenance and community adoption are unproven.

projects.name: DeepSeek-Reasonix
projects.description: DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for your terminal. Engineered around prefix-cache stability — leave it running.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for the terminal, rewritten in Go for performance and prefix-cache stability.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Provides a configurable, plugin-driven AI coding assistant that runs as a single static binary, optimized for DeepSeek's prefix caching to reduce token costs during long sessions.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 21,330 stars, a 24-hour delta of +481, and a 7-day average delta of 496.4, this project shows sustained high-velocity growth. The acceleration ratio of 0.9 indicates steady, organic interest rather than a sudden spike. It ranks top 2 in Agents and top 3 in LLMs by velocity on oosmetrics, signaling strong community adoption.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The recent 1.0 ground-up rewrite in Go (from TypeScript) is the major change, along with active commits and a release within 30 days. The README emphasizes the migration and new capabilities, likely driving renewed attention.
llmSummary.whyTrending: DeepSeek models are gaining traction as cost-effective alternatives to GPT-4, and Reasonix offers a polished, DeepSeek-first terminal agent with low friction (single binary, npm install). The Go rewrite improves performance and cross-platform support, appealing to developers seeking a lightweight, configurable coding assistant.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you use DeepSeek models and want a terminal-based coding agent with plugin support and prefix-cache optimization, Reasonix is worth trying. Its config-driven design and MCP-compatible plugin system make it extensible.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward model-specific, optimized agents that leverage provider-specific features (like DeepSeek's prefix cache) for cost efficiency. Developers should consider building agents that are tightly integrated with a single model's strengths rather than generic multi-model tools.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers who use DeepSeek models for coding assistance, prefer terminal-based tools, and want a configurable, plugin-extensible agent with low overhead.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Heavy reliance on DeepSeek's API and prefix-cache behavior; if DeepSeek changes pricing or caching, the value proposition may shift. The project is still relatively new (1.0 rewrite), so stability and long-term maintenance are unproven.

projects.name: open-design
projects.description: 🎨 Local-first, open-source Claude Design alternative. 🖥️ Native desktop app. ⚡ 259+ Skills · ✨ 142+ Design Systems 🖼️ Web · desktop · mobile prototypes · slides · images · videos · HyperFrames 📦 Sandboxed preview · HTML/PDF/PPTX/MP4 export 🤖 Claude Code / OpenClaw / Codex / Cursor / OpenCode / Qwen / Copilot / Hermes / Kimi & 17+ CLIs.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Open Design is a local-first, open-source alternative to Claude Design that turns your laptop into an agentic design studio with 259+ skills, 142+ design systems, and native desktop apps for macOS and Windows.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Design tools today are either closed-source SaaS (Figma, Claude Design) or require manual pixel-pushing. Open Design lets coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) generate prototypes, decks, images, and videos directly from a brief and a DESIGN.md brand contract, all running locally with sandboxed preview and export to HTML/PDF/PPTX/MP4.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 63,612 stars and a sustained 7-day average delta of 688.9 stars/day (24h delta: 653, acceleration: 1), Open Design is experiencing consistent, high-velocity growth. The recent 0.10.0 release introduces an all-in-one agentic design workspace with parallel sessions, making it feel like a local design team. This is not a flash-in-the-pan spike but a steady climb driven by real utility.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The 0.10.0 release (within 30 days) is the most likely trigger: it introduces an integrated workspace with parallel sessions, automation, and a new Agentic Model Router (AMR) service. The README also highlights a new 'Open Design Fellow' program, signaling community-building efforts.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It fills a gap for developers who want AI-generated design artifacts without leaving their coding agent or paying for closed SaaS. The BYOK model, support for 21+ CLI agents, and local-first approach resonate with the open-source and privacy-conscious developer community.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Open Design is a practical tool for developers who want to generate UI prototypes, decks, and videos directly from their coding agent (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) using a local-first, open-source stack. It's especially useful for teams that want to enforce brand consistency via DESIGN.md files.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward agent-native design workflows where the CLI becomes the design engine. Developers can now treat design as code, with version-controlled design systems and automated artifact generation. The MCP server integration makes it trivial to wire into existing agent setups.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers, designers, and product builders who use AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) and want to generate design artifacts (prototypes, decks, images, videos) locally without relying on closed SaaS tools like Figma or Claude Design.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is still in early stages (0.10.0) with 399 open issues. It depends on external AI model APIs and may have stability or compatibility issues. The rapid growth could also attract maintenance challenges. Additionally, the 'Claude Design alternative' positioning may face trademark or competitive risks.

projects.name: ai-engineering-from-scratch
projects.description: Learn it. Build it. Ship it for others.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A massive, free, open-source curriculum (503 lessons, 20 phases) that teaches AI engineering from math foundations to production-grade agents, shipping a reusable artifact per lesson.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Most AI learning resources are scattered, shallow, or API-only. This curriculum provides a structured, end-to-end path that builds deep understanding by implementing algorithms from scratch before using frameworks, closing the gap between AI tool usage and professional readiness.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 31,049 stars, a 7-day delta of 3,156 (avg 1,931/day), and a 24h delta of 294, this repo is experiencing sustained explosive growth. The acceleration of 0.4 indicates high but stable velocity, not a sudden spike. The project has 150,639 readers and 241,669 page views in the last 30 days, signaling massive community adoption. It's trending because it fills a clear gap: practical, deep AI education that produces real artifacts.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The README was recently updated (pushed 2026-06-10) and the curriculum is actively maintained with recent commits. The high star velocity over 7 days suggests ongoing word-of-mouth and social sharing rather than a single event.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It addresses the pain point that 84% of students use AI tools but only 18% feel prepared professionally. The curriculum's scope (503 lessons, 4 languages, 20 phases) and its 'build from scratch' philosophy resonate with developers who want genuine understanding. The built-in agent skills (/find-your-level, /check-understanding) and reusable artifacts (prompts, skills, agents, MCP servers) make it immediately useful.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This is a comprehensive, free resource for any developer wanting to deeply understand AI engineering. It's not just a tutorial — it's a structured curriculum that produces a portfolio of 503 artifacts. The inclusion of Rust, Julia, and TypeScript alongside Python broadens its appeal.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The market is shifting from API-wrapping to deep understanding. Developers who can build from scratch and ship production artifacts will have a significant advantage. This repo signals that the community values rigorous, hands-on learning over quick demos.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and engineers who want to understand AI deeply, from math to production. Students preparing for AI careers. Professionals transitioning into AI engineering. Anyone tired of surface-level tutorials and wanting to build real, shippable AI systems.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The curriculum is massive (320 hours), which may overwhelm some users. It's a solo project with no recent release, so long-term maintenance depends on the creator. The 'from scratch' approach may be too slow for those needing quick deployment skills.

projects.name: code-review-graph
projects.description: Local-first code intelligence graph for MCP and CLI. Builds a persistent map of your codebase so AI coding tools read only what matters, with benchmarked context reductions on reviews and large-repo workflows.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Local-first code intelligence graph that slashes AI token usage by ~82x median during code reviews via MCP and CLI.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI coding tools waste tokens by re-reading entire codebases on every review task. code-review-graph builds a persistent, incremental AST graph (Tree-sitter + SQLite) that computes the minimal blast radius of changes, so AI assistants read only relevant files.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 18,337 stars, a 24h delta of +46, and a 7-day average of +76.2 stars/day, this project shows sustained high-velocity growth. The acceleration of 0.6 (moderate) indicates steady organic interest rather than a viral spike, making it a reliable tool for the AI coding ecosystem.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. Recent commits (within 7 days) and a release within 30 days suggest active maintenance, and the README mentions broad platform support (Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) that likely drives ongoing adoption.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It directly addresses the token-cost pain point for AI-assisted development, offers a local-first privacy guarantee, and integrates seamlessly with major AI coding tools via MCP. The benchmarked 38x–528x token reduction is a compelling, quantifiable value proposition.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you use AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, etc.) on medium-to-large repos, this tool can dramatically cut token costs and speed up reviews. The one-command install and GitHub Action make it easy to adopt.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward local-first, context-aware AI tooling that minimizes API costs and latency. Developers building AI coding agents should consider integrating similar graph-based context pruning to stay competitive.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and teams using AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) on codebases of any size, especially monorepos or projects with frequent code reviews.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Project is relatively new (v2.x) with 122 open issues; dependency on Tree-sitter grammars may limit language coverage for niche languages. The 0.71 average impact F1 suggests room for improvement in relevance accuracy.

watchlistMovements.name: agent-skills
watchlistMovements.description: Production-grade engineering skills for AI coding agents.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A production-grade pack of 24 structured skills and slash commands that encode senior engineering workflows for AI coding agents.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI coding agents often produce inconsistent, low-quality output because they lack the structured workflows, quality gates, and best practices that senior engineers follow. This repository packages those practices into reusable, agent-executable skills.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo has exploded from 0 to 51,894 stars with a 24h delta of 2,051 and a 7d delta of 3,844. The acceleration ratio is 4 (high), indicating a sudden breakout. This is not gradual growth — it's a viral spike driven by the release of a comprehensive, well-documented skill pack for multiple agent platforms.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The repository was created recently and has seen a rapid initial burst of stars. The README describes a full set of 24 skills and 7 slash commands, with setup instructions for Claude Code, Cursor, Antigravity, Gemini CLI, Windsurf, OpenCode, GitHub Copilot, Kiro, and Codex. The sudden breakout suggests a launch announcement or viral social media post by the author (Addy Osmani, a well-known Google Chrome engineer) or coverage on Hacker News / Twitter.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It directly addresses a pain point for developers using AI coding agents: inconsistent output quality. By providing ready-made, production-grade skills that work across multiple agent platforms, it lowers the barrier to adopting structured agent workflows. The author's reputation and the comprehensive documentation accelerate adoption.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This is a practical, immediately usable resource for any developer using AI coding agents. You can install it in minutes and get consistent, senior-engineer-level behavior from your agent across the entire development lifecycle.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The project signals a shift from ad-hoc agent prompting to structured, skill-based agent programming. It suggests that the future of AI-assisted development lies in reusable, verifiable skill packs rather than raw LLM prompts. Developers should consider adopting or contributing to such skill ecosystems.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and teams using AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI, Copilot, etc.) who want to enforce consistent, high-quality engineering practices without manual prompting.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The skills are opinionated and may not fit every team's workflow. Over-reliance on predefined skills could reduce flexibility. The project is very new (no release history), so long-term maintenance and community adoption are unproven.

watchlistMovements.name: DeepSeek-Reasonix
watchlistMovements.description: DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for your terminal. Engineered around prefix-cache stability — leave it running.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for the terminal, rewritten in Go for performance and prefix-cache stability.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Provides a configurable, plugin-driven AI coding assistant that runs as a single static binary, optimized for DeepSeek's prefix caching to reduce token costs during long sessions.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 21,330 stars, a 24-hour delta of +481, and a 7-day average delta of 496.4, this project shows sustained high-velocity growth. The acceleration ratio of 0.9 indicates steady, organic interest rather than a sudden spike. It ranks top 2 in Agents and top 3 in LLMs by velocity on oosmetrics, signaling strong community adoption.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The recent 1.0 ground-up rewrite in Go (from TypeScript) is the major change, along with active commits and a release within 30 days. The README emphasizes the migration and new capabilities, likely driving renewed attention.
llmSummary.whyTrending: DeepSeek models are gaining traction as cost-effective alternatives to GPT-4, and Reasonix offers a polished, DeepSeek-first terminal agent with low friction (single binary, npm install). The Go rewrite improves performance and cross-platform support, appealing to developers seeking a lightweight, configurable coding assistant.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you use DeepSeek models and want a terminal-based coding agent with plugin support and prefix-cache optimization, Reasonix is worth trying. Its config-driven design and MCP-compatible plugin system make it extensible.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward model-specific, optimized agents that leverage provider-specific features (like DeepSeek's prefix cache) for cost efficiency. Developers should consider building agents that are tightly integrated with a single model's strengths rather than generic multi-model tools.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers who use DeepSeek models for coding assistance, prefer terminal-based tools, and want a configurable, plugin-extensible agent with low overhead.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Heavy reliance on DeepSeek's API and prefix-cache behavior; if DeepSeek changes pricing or caching, the value proposition may shift. The project is still relatively new (1.0 rewrite), so stability and long-term maintenance are unproven.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A massive, free, open-source curriculum (503 lessons, 20 phases) that teaches AI engineering from math foundations to production-grade agents, shipping a reusable artifact per lesson.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Most AI learning resources are scattered, shallow, or API-only. This curriculum provides a structured, end-to-end path that builds deep understanding by implementing algorithms from scratch before using frameworks, closing the gap between AI tool usage and professional readiness.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 31,049 stars, a 7-day delta of 3,156 (avg 1,931/day), and a 24h delta of 294, this repo is experiencing sustained explosive growth. The acceleration of 0.4 indicates high but stable velocity, not a sudden spike. The project has 150,639 readers and 241,669 page views in the last 30 days, signaling massive community adoption. It's trending because it fills a clear gap: practical, deep AI education that produces real artifacts.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The README was recently updated (pushed 2026-06-10) and the curriculum is actively maintained with recent commits. The high star velocity over 7 days suggests ongoing word-of-mouth and social sharing rather than a single event.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It addresses the pain point that 84% of students use AI tools but only 18% feel prepared professionally. The curriculum's scope (503 lessons, 4 languages, 20 phases) and its 'build from scratch' philosophy resonate with developers who want genuine understanding. The built-in agent skills (/find-your-level, /check-understanding) and reusable artifacts (prompts, skills, agents, MCP servers) make it immediately useful.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This is a comprehensive, free resource for any developer wanting to deeply understand AI engineering. It's not just a tutorial — it's a structured curriculum that produces a portfolio of 503 artifacts. The inclusion of Rust, Julia, and TypeScript alongside Python broadens its appeal.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The market is shifting from API-wrapping to deep understanding. Developers who can build from scratch and ship production artifacts will have a significant advantage. This repo signals that the community values rigorous, hands-on learning over quick demos.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and engineers who want to understand AI deeply, from math to production. Students preparing for AI careers. Professionals transitioning into AI engineering. Anyone tired of surface-level tutorials and wanting to build real, shippable AI systems.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The curriculum is massive (320 hours), which may overwhelm some users. It's a solo project with no recent release, so long-term maintenance depends on the creator. The 'from scratch' approach may be too slow for those needing quick deployment skills.

watchlistMovements.name: code-review-graph
watchlistMovements.description: Local-first code intelligence graph for MCP and CLI. Builds a persistent map of your codebase so AI coding tools read only what matters, with benchmarked context reductions on reviews and large-repo workflows.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Local-first code intelligence graph that slashes AI token usage by ~82x median during code reviews via MCP and CLI.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI coding tools waste tokens by re-reading entire codebases on every review task. code-review-graph builds a persistent, incremental AST graph (Tree-sitter + SQLite) that computes the minimal blast radius of changes, so AI assistants read only relevant files.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 18,337 stars, a 24h delta of +46, and a 7-day average of +76.2 stars/day, this project shows sustained high-velocity growth. The acceleration of 0.6 (moderate) indicates steady organic interest rather than a viral spike, making it a reliable tool for the AI coding ecosystem.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. Recent commits (within 7 days) and a release within 30 days suggest active maintenance, and the README mentions broad platform support (Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) that likely drives ongoing adoption.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It directly addresses the token-cost pain point for AI-assisted development, offers a local-first privacy guarantee, and integrates seamlessly with major AI coding tools via MCP. The benchmarked 38x–528x token reduction is a compelling, quantifiable value proposition.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you use AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, etc.) on medium-to-large repos, this tool can dramatically cut token costs and speed up reviews. The one-command install and GitHub Action make it easy to adopt.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward local-first, context-aware AI tooling that minimizes API costs and latency. Developers building AI coding agents should consider integrating similar graph-based context pruning to stay competitive.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and teams using AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) on codebases of any size, especially monorepos or projects with frequent code reviews.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Project is relatively new (v2.x) with 122 open issues; dependency on Tree-sitter grammars may limit language coverage for niche languages. The 0.71 average impact F1 suggests room for improvement in relevance accuracy.

modelDemoSignals.title: EvoArena: Tracking Memory Evolution for Robust LLM Agents in Dynamic Environments
modelDemoSignals.description: Large language model (LLM) agents have achieved strong performance on a wide range of benchmarks, yet most evaluations assume static environments. In contrast, real-world deployment is inherently dynamic, requiring agents to continually align their knowledge, skills, and behavior with changing environments and updated task conditions. To address this gap, we introduce EvoArena, a benchmark suite that models environment changes as sequences of progressive updates across terminal, software, and so
modelDemoSignals.summary: Large language model (LLM) agents have achieved strong performance on a wide range of benchmarks, yet most evaluations assume static environments. In contrast, real-world deployment is inherently dynamic, requiring agents to continually align their knowledge, skills, and behavior with changing environments and updated task conditions. To address this gap, we introduce EvoArena, a benchmark suite that models environment changes as sequences of progressive updates across terminal, software, and social domains. We further propose EvoMem, a patch-based memory paradigm that records memory evolution as structured update histories, enabling agents to reason about environmental evolution through changes in their memory. Experiments show that current agents struggle on EvoArena, achieving an average accuracy of 39.6% across evolving terminal, software, and social-preference domains. EvoMem consistently improves performance, yielding an average gain of 1.5% on EvoArena and also improving standard benchmarks such as GAIA and LoCoMo by 6.1% and 4.8%. Beyond individual tasks, EvoMem further improves chain-level accuracy by 3.7% on EvoArena, where success requires completing a consecutive sequence of related evolutionary subtasks. Mechanistic analysis shows that EvoMem improves evidence capture in the memory, indicating better preservation of complete evolving environment states. Our results highlight the importance of modeling evolution in both evaluation and memory for reliable agent deployment.
modelDemoSignals.author: Jundong Xu, Qingchuan Li, Jiaying Wu, Yihuai Lan, Shuyue Stella Li, Huichi Zhou, Bowen Jiang, Lei Wang, Jun Wang, Anh Tuan Luu, Caiming Xiong, Hae Won Park, Bryan Hooi, Zhiyuan Hu
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, agent, rag, benchmark

modelDemoSignals.title: Learning to Reason by Analogy via Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
modelDemoSignals.description: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) has become a standard mechanism for grounding language models in external knowledge, yet conventional retrieval based on lexical or semantic similarity is poorly suited for complex reasoning tasks: a semantically similar problem may demand an entirely different solution strategy, while a superficially different problem may share the same underlying reasoning pattern. We propose Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT), a post-training framework
modelDemoSignals.summary: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) has become a standard mechanism for grounding language models in external knowledge, yet conventional retrieval based on lexical or semantic similarity is poorly suited for complex reasoning tasks: a semantically similar problem may demand an entirely different solution strategy, while a superficially different problem may share the same underlying reasoning pattern. We propose Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT), a post-training framework that teaches language models to reason by analogy. RA-RFT uses gold-relevance distillation to train a retriever that ranks contexts by expected reasoning benefit rather than semantic overlap, and then fine-tunes the policy model via reinforcement fine-tuning methods with retrieved analogous demonstrations, so the model learns to leverage reasoning traces under verifiable outcome rewards. We further analyze the diversity of retrieved contexts and find that reasoning-aware retrieval surfaces complementary solution strategies that provide distinct reasoning scaffolds for individual problems. Across challenging mathematical reasoning benchmarks, RA-RFT consistently outperforms standard reinforcement fine-tuning methods. For example, it improves AIME 2025 average@32 accuracy by 7.1 and 2.8 points over GRPO for Qwen3-1.7B and Qwen3-4B respectively -- suggesting that reasoning-aware retrieval is a complementary axis of improvement and orthogonal to advances in reward design or training curricula.
modelDemoSignals.author: Zilin Xiao, Qi Ma, Chun-cheng Jason Chen, Xintao Chen, Avinash Atreya, Hanjie Chen, Vicente Ordonez
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: retrieval-augmented, rag, fine-tuning, benchmark

modelDemoSignals.description: Articulated tool manipulation remains a major challenge in dexterous robotics due to the need to coordinate internal degrees of freedom and contact-rich interactions. modelDemoSignals.summary: Articulated tool manipulation remains a major challenge in dexterous robotics due to the need to coordinate internal degrees of freedom and contact-rich interactions. While prior work has largely focused on rigid objects, articulated tool use remains underexplored because of its physical complexity and the difficulty of learning functional grasping and manipulation policies. We present Mana (Manipulation Animator), a general sim-to-real framework that reinterprets dexterous manipulation as an animation problem. Inspired by computer animation, Mana employs a coarse-to-fine pipeline that transforms procedurally-generated grasp keyframes into manipulation trajectories through motion planning and reinforcement learning. The data generation process is largely automatic, requiring only a few mouse clicks to specify functional affordances (<1 minute per tool). Across four articulated tools spanning different scales and joint types, Mana achieves zero-shot sim-to-real transfer for both grasping and in-hand manipulation, demonstrating a scalable approach to dexterous articulated tool use.
modelDemoSignals.author: Zhao-Heng Yin, Guanya Shi, Pieter Abbeel, C. Karen Liu
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: tool use

modelDemoSignals.title: SpatialClaw: Rethinking Action Interface for Agentic Spatial Reasoning
modelDemoSignals.description: Spatial reasoning, the ability to determine where objects are, how they relate, and how they move in 3D, remains a fundamental challenge for vision-language models (VLMs). Tool-augmented agents attempt to address this by augmenting VLMs with specialist perception modules, yet their effectiveness is bounded by the action interface through which those tools are invoked. In this work, we study how the design of this interface shapes the agent's capacity for open-ended spatial reasoning. Existing sp
modelDemoSignals.summary: Spatial reasoning, the ability to determine where objects are, how they relate, and how they move in 3D, remains a fundamental challenge for vision-language models (VLMs). Tool-augmented agents attempt to address this by augmenting VLMs with specialist perception modules, yet their effectiveness is bounded by the action interface through which those tools are invoked. In this work, we study how the design of this interface shapes the agent's capacity for open-ended spatial reasoning. Existing spatial agents either employ single-pass code execution, which commits to a full analysis strategy before any intermediate result is observed, or rely on a structured tool-call interface that often offers less flexibility for freely composing operations or tailoring the analysis to each task. Both designs offer limited flexibility for open-ended, complex 3D/4D spatial reasoning. We therefore propose SpatialClaw, a training-free framework for spatial reasoning that adopts code as the action interface. SpatialClaw maintains a stateful Python kernel pre-loaded with input frames and a suite of perception and geometry primitives, letting a VLM-backed agent write one executable cell per step conditioned on all prior outputs, enabling the agent to flexibly compose and manipulate perception results and adapt its analysis to both intermediate text and visual observations and the demands of each problem. Evaluated across 20 spatial reasoning benchmarks spanning a broad range of static and dynamic 3D/4D spatial reasoning tasks, SpatialClaw achieves 59.9% average accuracy, outperforming the recent spatial agent by +11.2 points, with consistent gains across six VLM backbones from two model families without any benchmark- or model-specific adaptation.
modelDemoSignals.author: Seokju Cho, Ryo Hachiuma, Abhishek Badki, Hang Su, Byung-Kwan Lee, Chan Hee Song, Sifei Liu, Subhashree Radhakrishnan, Seungryong Kim, Yu-Chiang Frank Wang, Min-Hung Chen
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: agent, rag, benchmark, agentic

modelDemoSignals.title: Automated reproducibility assessments in the social and behavioral sciences using large language models
modelDemoSignals.description: Reproducibility in the social and behavioral sciences is typically evaluated by independent researchers who reanalyze the original data to assess whether the published findings can be recovered. However, such approaches are resource-intensive and difficult to scale. Here, we show that large language models (LLMs) can automate reproducibility assessments. Using N=76 published studies with predefined claims from the behavioral and social sciences, we compare LLM-generated analysis with the origina
modelDemoSignals.summary: Reproducibility in the social and behavioral sciences is typically evaluated by independent researchers who reanalyze the original data to assess whether the published findings can be recovered. However, such approaches are resource-intensive and difficult to scale. Here, we show that large language models (LLMs) can automate reproducibility assessments. Using N=76 published studies with predefined claims from the behavioral and social sciences, we compare LLM-generated analysis with the original findings and human reanalysis. For 7 studies, the LLM could not produce a viable effect size estimate. For the remaining studies, our LLM pipeline recovered the original effect sizes in 41% of studies using a +/-0.05 tolerance in Cohen's d. Further, our LLM pipeline reached the same qualitative conclusion as the original study in 96% of cases, where conclusions indicate whether the reanalysis supports the original claim. For comparison, human reanalysts recovered the original effect sizes in 34% of studies and reached the same qualitative conclusion in 74% of cases. Together, these results show that LLMs can serve as a scalable tool for automated reproducibility assessment and provide a foundation for systematic auditing of empirical results in the social and behavioral sciences.
modelDemoSignals.author: Tobias Holtdirk, Pietro Marcolongo, Anna Steinberg Schulten, Felix Henninger, Stefan Rose, Sarah Ball, Bolei Ma, Frauke Kreuter, Markus Weinmann, Stefan Feuerriegel
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm

items.title: 小互开源公众号自动排版技能组合
items.description: 小互（@xiaohu）升级了公众号排版技能组合，实现一句话完成排版、封面生成并一键发送到公众号草稿箱。该工具已开源，提供20种主题颜色可选，可自动分析内容进行排版，支持非Markdown文件。用户只需在Claude Code、Codex或OpenClaw中提供文章链接或文档位置，即可获得可视化预览界面进行选择，全程无需手动操作。

items.description: 推文提出AI Agent开发中人类与AI对PRD的需求不同，为此发布了一个专门服务于AI的PRD文档生成Prompt（命名为qiaomu-ai-prd）。开发者先使用该Prompt生成文档，再交给AI开发，可显著提升功能完整度和丰富性。安装指令为：`npx skills add joeseesun/qiaomu-ai-prd`，开源地址及Prompt见评论区。

items.title: 苹果 iOS 27 健康 App 大改：卡片布局、营养识别、围绝经期追踪
items.description: 苹果在 iOS 27 中优化健康 App，将列表改为卡片布局并增加导航栏。新增视觉智能营养识别，用户通过相机 Siri 模式拍摄食物可获取加工程度、蛋白质、含糖量等信息及营养价值评级，不提供精确卡路里，需 iPhone 15 Pro 及以上。经期追踪扩展支持围绝经期，可分析长期周期异常模式并推送提醒与指导。Fitness+ 新增围绝经期和绝经期课程。数据同步速度提升，GymKit 扩展至 iPhone，无需 Apple Watch 即可与健身设备配对同步数据。

items.title: Spec 驱动开发（SDD）的三个 Skills：覆盖 Spec→Implement→Verify 闭环
items.description: 邵猛分享 Spec 驱动开发（SDD）方法，用三个 Skills（/write-product-spec、/write-tech-spec、/validate-changes-match-specs）覆盖 Spec→Implement→Verify 闭环。规格分两层：PRODUCT.md（用户故事、不变量）和 TECH.md（架构、实现策略），均放在 specs/<issue>/ 目录，随 PR 提交。五步流程：写产品规格、写技术规格、Agent 按规格实现、一致性校验、计算机操作端到端验证。Skills 可移植，不绑定 Warp。开源仓库 warpdotdev/common-skills，安装：npx skills add warpdotdev/common-skills。

trendEntities.summary: Production-grade engineering skills for AI coding agents.

llmSummary.whatItIs: A curated collection of production-grade engineering skills designed to enhance AI coding agents, created by Google Chrome engineer Addy Osmani.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repository has seen a massive surge in GitHub stars (55k+ total, 3.3k in 24h, 6.9k in 7d), indicating strong developer interest in practical, high-quality resources for improving AI agent capabilities.
llmSummary.whoShouldCare: AI/ML engineers, developer tooling teams, and platform engineers building or integrating AI coding agents.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: Highlights the growing demand for production-ready AI agent skills, which can accelerate development workflows and reduce engineering overhead.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Provides actionable, curated skills that developers can directly apply to improve their AI coding agents' performance and reliability.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Stars reflect attention, not production adoption. The repo is new (Feb 2026) and may evolve rapidly. No evidence of widespread enterprise use yet.

trendEntities.summary: DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for your terminal. Engineered around prefix-cache stability — leave it running.

llmSummary.whatItIs: DeepSeek-Reasonix is an open-source terminal-based AI coding agent built on DeepSeek models, emphasizing prefix-cache stability for persistent use.
llmSummary.whyNow: Rapid star growth (3,189 in 7 days) indicates strong developer interest in DeepSeek-native coding agents, especially those designed for long-running terminal sessions.

llmSummary.whatItIs: A CLI proxy that intercepts LLM API calls and reduces token usage by 60-90% via caching, compression, and prompt optimization, implemented as a single Rust binary with zero dependencies.
llmSummary.whyNow: Sustained high star growth (2,625 stars in 7 days) and a high heat score (420) indicate strong developer interest in cost optimization for LLM usage, especially as token costs remain a pain point.
llmSummary.whoShouldCare: Developers building AI-powered tools, DevOps engineers managing LLM API costs, product managers at AI startups, and platform teams optimizing infrastructure for LLM calls.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: Directly addresses the growing need to reduce LLM API costs, which is a top concern for companies scaling AI features. The tool's simplicity (single binary) lowers adoption friction.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Provides an immediate, measurable way to cut token consumption without code changes. Developers can integrate it as a proxy to existing workflows and see cost savings quickly.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Star growth may be inflated by novelty and social media buzz; production adoption and long-term maintenance are unproven. Token reduction claims may vary by use case.

sourceItems.summary: 小互（@xiaohu）升级了公众号排版技能组合，实现一句话完成排版、封面生成并一键发送到公众号草稿箱。该工具已开源，提供20种主题颜色可选，可自动分析内容进行排版，支持非Markdown文件。用户只需在Claude Code、Codex或OpenClaw中提供文章链接或文档位置，即可获得可视化预览界面进行选择，全程无需手动操作。

items.title: qiaomu-ai-prd：面向AI的PRD生成Prompt
items.summary: 推文提出AI Agent开发中人类与AI对PRD的需求不同，为此发布了一个专门服务于AI的PRD文档生成Prompt（命名为qiaomu-ai-prd）。开发者先使用该Prompt生成文档，再交给AI开发，可显著提升功能完整度和丰富性。安装指令为：`npx skills add joeseesun/qiaomu-ai-prd`，开源地址及Prompt见评论区。

trendEntities.title: 苹果 iOS 27 健康 App 大改：卡片布局、营养识别、围绝经期追踪
normalizedKeys: title:苹果 ios 27 健康 app 大改 卡片布局 营养识别 围绝经期追踪

items.title: 苹果 iOS 27 健康 App 大改：卡片布局、营养识别、围绝经期追踪
items.summary: 苹果在 iOS 27 中优化健康 App，将列表改为卡片布局并增加导航栏。新增视觉智能营养识别，用户通过相机 Siri 模式拍摄食物可获取加工程度、蛋白质、含糖量等信息及营养价值评级，不提供精确卡路里，需 iPhone 15 Pro 及以上。经期追踪扩展支持围绝经期，可分析长期周期异常模式并推送提醒与指导。Fitness+ 新增围绝经期和绝经期课程。数据同步速度提升，GymKit 扩展至 iPhone，无需 Apple Watch 即可与健身设备配对同步数据。
items.description: 苹果在 iOS 27 中优化健康 App，将列表改为卡片布局并增加导航栏。新增视觉智能营养识别，用户通过相机 Siri 模式拍摄食物可获取加工程度、蛋白质、含糖量等信息及营养价值评级，不提供精确卡路里，需 iPhone 15 Pro 及以上。经期追踪扩展支持围绝经期，可分析长期周期异常模式并推送提醒与指导。Fitness+ 新增围绝经期和绝经期课程。数据同步速度提升，GymKit 扩展至 iPhone，无需 Apple Watch 即可与健身设备配对同步数据。

trendEntities.title: Spec 驱动开发（SDD）的三个 Skills：覆盖 Spec→Implement→Verify 闭环
normalizedKeys: title:spec 驱动开发 sdd 的三个 skills 覆盖 spec implement verify 闭环

items.title: Spec 驱动开发（SDD）的三个 Skills：覆盖 Spec→Implement→Verify 闭环
items.summary: 邵猛分享 Spec 驱动开发（SDD）方法，用三个 Skills（/write-product-spec、/write-tech-spec、/validate-changes-match-specs）覆盖 Spec→Implement→Verify 闭环。规格分两层：PRODUCT.md（用户故事、不变量）和 TECH.md（架构、实现策略），均放在 specs/<issue>/ 目录，随 PR 提交。五步流程：写产品规格、写技术规格、Agent 按规格实现、一致性校验、计算机操作端到端验证。Skills 可移植，不绑定 Warp。开源仓库 warpdotdev/common-skills，安装：npx skills add warpdotdev/common-skills。
items.description: 邵猛分享 Spec 驱动开发（SDD）方法，用三个 Skills（/write-product-spec、/write-tech-spec、/validate-changes-match-specs）覆盖 Spec→Implement→Verify 闭环。规格分两层：PRODUCT.md（用户故事、不变量）和 TECH.md（架构、实现策略），均放在 specs/<issue>/ 目录，随 PR 提交。五步流程：写产品规格、写技术规格、Agent 按规格实现、一致性校验、计算机操作端到端验证。Skills 可移植，不绑定 Warp。开源仓库 warpdotdev/common-skills，安装：npx skills add warpdotdev/common-skills。

items.title: Codex 推出浏览器开发者模式
items.summary: 为 Chrome 和 Codex 内置浏览器引入开发者模式。 Codex 可以使用 Chrome DevTools 协议（CDP）来调试浏览器问题，通过分析 JavaScript 性能、检查控制台输出、网络流量和页面状态。

items.title: Replit Agent 新增自定义指令与技能功能
items.summary: AI 智能体很强大，但它们不记得你的偏好。 所以你总是重复指令--如何组织项目、你的品牌指南。 现在你可以通过自定义指令和技能让 Replit Agent 学会你的惯例。 它会在每个项目中自动将这些考虑进去。

trendEntities.title: Anthropic与DXC达成全球联盟，将Claude引入关键行业系统
normalizedKeys: title:anthropic与dxc达成全球联盟 将claude引入关键行业系统

items.title: Anthropic与DXC达成全球联盟，将Claude引入关键行业系统
items.summary: Anthropic与IT服务公司DXC Technology达成多年全球联盟。DXC将培训数万名获得Claude认证的前沿部署工程师（FDE），将Claude引入其为全球大型银行、航空公司、保险公司及政府机构运营的关键系统。内部部署中，Claude已成为DXC OASIS平台的默认基础模型，该平台超95%代码由Claude编写，开发速度提升10倍，已服务50多家客户。DXC加入Claude Partner Network，将在保险、现代化服务、网络安全及应用服务四个领域率先推出基于Claude的解决方案。

sourceItems.summary: Anthropic与IT服务公司DXC Technology达成多年全球联盟。DXC将培训数万名获得Claude认证的前沿部署工程师（FDE），将Claude引入其为全球大型银行、航空公司、保险公司及政府机构运营的关键系统。内部部署中，Claude已成为DXC OASIS平台的默认基础模型，该平台超95%代码由Claude编写，开发速度提升10倍，已服务50多家客户。DXC加入Claude Partner Network，将在保险、现代化服务、网络安全及应用服务四个领域率先推出基于Claude的解决方案。

trendEntities.title: Prometheus 融资120亿美元，估值410亿美元，定位"人工通用工程师"
normalizedKeys: title:prometheus 融资120亿美元 估值410亿美元 定位 人工通用工程师

items.title: Prometheus 融资120亿美元，估值410亿美元，定位"人工通用工程师"
items.summary: 杰夫·贝佐斯旗下AI公司Prometheus在成立仅7个月、尚无任何产品交付的情况下，以410亿美元估值完成120亿美元融资（最初估值62亿美元）。该公司定位为"人工通用工程师"，目标是将设计到制造的循环压缩10倍以上。但物理经济无法像互联网数据那样抓取，缺乏制造业训练数据。为此，Prometheus计划斥资1000亿美元收购传统工业企业，通过获取工厂生成的数据构建护城河。

trendEntities.title: Codex Goal指令生成Skill发布：一句话需求转目标
normalizedKeys: title:codex goal指令生成skill发布 一句话需求转目标

items.title: Codex Goal指令生成Skill发布：一句话需求转目标
items.summary: 针对如何给Codex写Goal指令的问题，作者发布了一个Skill，可将一句话需求自动转化为目标，实现"睡前写指令、模型自动开发、第二天收菜"。安装命令：`npx skills add joeseesun/qiaomu-goal-meta-skill`。源码免费开源（见评论区），旨在简化4w字文档的阅读负担。

trendEntities.title: Runway与Lionsgate扩大战略合作

items.title: Runway与Lionsgate扩大战略合作
items.summary: 全球内容巨头Lionsgate与生成式AI研究公司Runway今日宣布扩大战略合作。Lionsgate已取得Runway股权，双方将启动联合开发项目，共同创作并制作新IP，首推一部基于Lionsgate现有IP和Runway生成模型的短剧系列。此外，Lionsgate将作为主持伙伴参与6月举行的Runway AI电影节。这是双方继2024年9月首次合作后的深化，Lionsgate此前已在预可视化、故事板及最终帧制作中应用Runway工具。

trendEntities.title: Anthropic 启动 Claude Corps 全国奖学金项目
normalizedKeys: title:anthropic 启动 claude corps 全国奖学金项目

items.title: Anthropic 启动 Claude Corps 全国奖学金项目
items.summary: Anthropic 推出 Claude Corps 奖学金项目，面向早期职业生涯的年轻人，培训 1，000 名研究员使用 Claude，并匹配给美国非营利组织全职工作一年。研究员年薪 85，000 美元及福利，每周接受 5 小时持续培训。Anthropic 初始投入 1.5 亿美元，合作方为 CodePath 和 Social Finance。至少 400 家非营利组织将参与接待，包括 Braven、Code the Dream、Heartland Forward 等。

trendEntities.title: Cursor 推出 Auto-review 机制：用分类器智能体动态管控智能体自主权限
normalizedKeys: title:cursor 推出 auto review 机制 用分类器智能体动态管控智能体自主权限

items.title: Cursor 推出 Auto-review 机制：用分类器智能体动态管控智能体自主权限
items.summary: Cursor 近日推出 Auto-review，通过一个专门的分类器智能体在工具调用前审查动作风险。该分类器根据上下文判断动作是否与用户意图一致，高风险时阻止并返回解释给父智能体，低风险时放行。分类器采用小模型，运行在智能体循环内以避免额外延迟，并能读取工作区文件辅助判断。测试基于约12小时内部开发会话生成的6122条标签数据，以及针对读取密钥、操作生产数据等危险场景的合成数据。设计目标是在不频繁阻断日常开发的前提下，拦截风险动作。

items.title: Codex 维护仓库：5分钟循环并行自治

trendEntities.title: 阿里云发布 Meoo CLI：本地 AI 编程项目可一键部署上线
normalizedKeys: title:阿里云发布 meoo cli 本地 ai 编程项目可一键部署上线

items.title: 阿里云发布 Meoo CLI：本地 AI 编程项目可一键部署上线
items.summary: 阿里云今日推出 Meoo（秒悟）开源命令行工具 Meoo CLI，面向开发者，支持 Claude Code、Codex、Cursor 等本地 AI 编程助手。通过调用阿里云云端能力，可自动完成数据库接入、用户登录、文件存储及项目发布，将本地项目一键部署上线并生成可分享的访问链接。该工具定位为本地 Agent 与秒悟云端能力的连接入口，已开放下载，支持主流 Linux、macOS 及 Windows 平台。

trendEntities.title: 千问推出首个足球预测AI助手，竞猜赢奖并捐建球场
normalizedKeys: title:千问推出首个足球预测ai助手 竞猜赢奖并捐建球场

items.title: 千问推出首个足球预测AI助手，竞猜赢奖并捐建球场
items.summary: 千问上线首个足球预测AI助手，基于海量大数据（含历史比赛、球员数据、伤病、美加墨地貌及天气等）。以6月22日挪威对塞内加尔为例，预测"1：1平局"，理由为气候差异。活动：参与全部104场竞猜，预测超80场且准确率超千问可抽万元大奖（100个名额）；预测超32场可抽千问AI眼镜G1（1000副），该眼镜支持赛后分析、拍屏识球员及赛事结果订阅。累积积分将向乡村学校捐建足球场，目标至少50所。

trendEntities.title: 腾讯混元 AI Infra 新开源：HPC-Ops 推理核心算子全面升级
normalizedKeys: title:腾讯混元 ai infra 新开源 hpc ops 推理核心算子全面升级

items.title: 腾讯混元 AI Infra 新开源：HPC-Ops 推理核心算子全面升级
items.summary: 腾讯混元 AI Infra 团队开源升级 HPC-Ops 推理算子库，推出五大核心算子。Attention 采用运行时动态负载调度，长文本最高加速 2.95x，端到端 QPM 提升 17%；Router GEMM 以双 BF16 组合实现 FP32 精度，对比 CuBLAS FP32 最高提速 3.22x；FusedMoE 相对 vLLM、SGLang 性能提升 1.2x~1.6x；Fused AllReduce+Norm 对比主流方案最高提速 1.68x；Sampler 将解码采样融合为 2 个 CUDA Kernel，相对 vLLM 提速 4.0x~7.5x。所有能力均来自生产实践并完全开源。

trendEntities.title: EvoArena: Tracking Memory Evolution for Robust LLM Agents in Dynamic Environments
normalizedKeys: title:evoarena tracking memory evolution for robust llm agents in dynamic environments

items.title: EvoArena: Tracking Memory Evolution for Robust LLM Agents in Dynamic Environments
items.description: Large language model (LLM) agents have achieved strong performance on a wide range of benchmarks, yet most evaluations assume static environments. In contrast, real-world deployment is inherently dynamic, requiring agents to continually align their knowledge, skills, and behavior with changing environments and updated task conditions. items.summary: Large language model (LLM) agents have achieved strong performance on a wide range of benchmarks, yet most evaluations assume static environments. In contrast, real-world deployment is inherently dynamic, requiring agents to continually align their knowledge, skills, and behavior with changing environments and updated task conditions. To address this gap, we introduce EvoArena, a benchmark suite that models environment changes as sequences of progressive updates across terminal, software, and social domains. We further propose EvoMem, a patch-based memory paradigm that records memory evolution as structured update histories, enabling agents to reason about environmental evolution through changes in their memory. Experiments show that current agents struggle on EvoArena, achieving an average accuracy of 39.6% across evolving terminal, software, and social-preference domains. EvoMem consistently improves performance, yielding an average gain of 1.5% on EvoArena and also improving standard benchmarks such as GAIA and LoCoMo by 6.1% and 4.8%. Beyond individual tasks, EvoMem further improves chain-level accuracy by 3.7% on EvoArena, where success requires completing a consecutive sequence of related evolutionary subtasks. Mechanistic analysis shows that EvoMem improves evidence capture in the memory, indicating better preservation of complete evolving environment states. Our results highlight the importance of modeling evolution in both evaluation and memory for reliable agent deployment.
items.author: Jundong Xu, Qingchuan Li, Jiaying Wu, Yihuai Lan, Shuyue Stella Li, Huichi Zhou, Bowen Jiang, Lei Wang, Jun Wang, Anh Tuan Luu, Caiming Xiong, Hae Won Park, Bryan Hooi, Zhiyuan Hu

In contrast, real-world deployment is inherently dynamic, requiring agents to continually align their knowledge, skills, and behavior with changing environments and updated task conditions. To address this gap, we introduce EvoArena, a benchmark suite that models environment changes as sequences of progressive updates across terminal, software, and social domains. Mechanistic analysis shows that EvoMem improves evidence capture in the memory, indicating better preservation of complete evolving environment states. Our results highlight the importance of modeling evolution in both evaluation and memory for reliable agent deployment.

trendEntities.title: Learning to Reason by Analogy via Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
normalizedKeys: title:learning to reason by analogy via retrieval augmented reinforcement fine tuning

items.title: Learning to Reason by Analogy via Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
items.description: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) has become a standard mechanism for grounding language models in external knowledge, yet conventional retrieval based on lexical or semantic similarity is poorly suited for complex reasoning tasks: a semantically similar problem may demand an entirely different solution strategy, while a superficially different problem may share the same underlying reasoning pattern. We propose Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT), a post-training framework
items.summary: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) has become a standard mechanism for grounding language models in external knowledge, yet conventional retrieval based on lexical or semantic similarity is poorly suited for complex reasoning tasks: a semantically similar problem may demand an entirely different solution strategy, while a superficially different problem may share the same underlying reasoning pattern. We propose Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT), a post-training framework that teaches language models to reason by analogy. RA-RFT uses gold-relevance distillation to train a retriever that ranks contexts by expected reasoning benefit rather than semantic overlap, and then fine-tunes the policy model via reinforcement fine-tuning methods with retrieved analogous demonstrations, so the model learns to leverage reasoning traces under verifiable outcome rewards. We further analyze the diversity of retrieved contexts and find that reasoning-aware retrieval surfaces complementary solution strategies that provide distinct reasoning scaffolds for individual problems. Across challenging mathematical reasoning benchmarks, RA-RFT consistently outperforms standard reinforcement fine-tuning methods. For example, it improves AIME 2025 average@32 accuracy by 7.1 and 2.8 points over GRPO for Qwen3-1.7B and Qwen3-4B respectively -- suggesting that reasoning-aware retrieval is a complementary axis of improvement and orthogonal to advances in reward design or training curricula.
items.author: Zilin Xiao, Qi Ma, Chun-cheng Jason Chen, Xintao Chen, Avinash Atreya, Hanjie Chen, Vicente Ordonez
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: retrieval-augmented, rag, fine-tuning, benchmark

trendEntities.title: Mana: Dexterous Manipulation of Articulated Tools
normalizedKeys: title:mana dexterous manipulation of articulated tools

items.title: Mana: Dexterous Manipulation of Articulated Tools
items.description: Articulated tool manipulation remains a major challenge in dexterous robotics due to the need to coordinate internal degrees of freedom and contact-rich interactions. While prior work has largely focused on rigid objects, articulated tool use remains underexplored because of its physical complexity and the difficulty of learning functional grasping and manipulation policies. items.summary: Articulated tool manipulation remains a major challenge in dexterous robotics due to the need to coordinate internal degrees of freedom and contact-rich interactions. While prior work has largely focused on rigid objects, articulated tool use remains underexplored because of its physical complexity and the difficulty of learning functional grasping and manipulation policies. We present Mana (Manipulation Animator), a general sim-to-real framework that reinterprets dexterous manipulation as an animation problem. Inspired by computer animation, Mana employs a coarse-to-fine pipeline that transforms procedurally-generated grasp keyframes into manipulation trajectories through motion planning and reinforcement learning. The data generation process is largely automatic, requiring only a few mouse clicks to specify functional affordances (<1 minute per tool). Across four articulated tools spanning different scales and joint types, Mana achieves zero-shot sim-to-real transfer for both grasping and in-hand manipulation, demonstrating a scalable approach to dexterous articulated tool use.
items.author: Zhao-Heng Yin, Guanya Shi, Pieter Abbeel, C. Karen Liu
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: tool use

items.title: SpatialClaw: Rethinking Action Interface for Agentic Spatial Reasoning
items.description: Spatial reasoning, the ability to determine where objects are, how they relate, and how they move in 3D, remains a fundamental challenge for vision-language models (VLMs). Tool-augmented agents attempt to address this by augmenting VLMs with specialist perception modules, yet their effectiveness is bounded by the action interface through which those tools are invoked. items.summary: Spatial reasoning, the ability to determine where objects are, how they relate, and how they move in 3D, remains a fundamental challenge for vision-language models (VLMs). Tool-augmented agents attempt to address this by augmenting VLMs with specialist perception modules, yet their effectiveness is bounded by the action interface through which those tools are invoked. In this work, we study how the design of this interface shapes the agent's capacity for open-ended spatial reasoning. Existing spatial agents either employ single-pass code execution, which commits to a full analysis strategy before any intermediate result is observed, or rely on a structured tool-call interface that often offers less flexibility for freely composing operations or tailoring the analysis to each task. Both designs offer limited flexibility for open-ended, complex 3D/4D spatial reasoning. We therefore propose SpatialClaw, a training-free framework for spatial reasoning that adopts code as the action interface. SpatialClaw maintains a stateful Python kernel pre-loaded with input frames and a suite of perception and geometry primitives, letting a VLM-backed agent write one executable cell per step conditioned on all prior outputs, enabling the agent to flexibly compose and manipulate perception results and adapt its analysis to both intermediate text and visual observations and the demands of each problem. Evaluated across 20 spatial reasoning benchmarks spanning a broad range of static and dynamic 3D/4D spatial reasoning tasks, SpatialClaw achieves 59.9% average accuracy, outperforming the recent spatial agent by +11.2 points, with consistent gains across six VLM backbones from two model families without any benchmark- or model-specific adaptation.
items.author: Seokju Cho, Ryo Hachiuma, Abhishek Badki, Hang Su, Byung-Kwan Lee, Chan Hee Song, Sifei Liu, Subhashree Radhakrishnan, Seungryong Kim, Yu-Chiang Frank Wang, Min-Hung Chen

Existing spatial agents either employ single-pass code execution, which commits to a full analysis strategy before any intermediate result is observed, or rely on a structured tool-call interface that often offers less flexibility for freely composing operations or tailoring the analysis to each task. Both designs offer limited flexibility for open-ended, complex 3D/4D spatial reasoning. We therefore propose SpatialClaw, a training-free framework for spatial reasoning that adopts code as the action interface. SpatialClaw maintains a stateful Python kernel pre-loaded with input frames and a suite of perception and geometry primitives, letting a VLM-backed agent write one executable cell per step conditioned on all prior outputs, enabling the agent to flexibly compose and manipulate perception results and adapt its analysis to both intermediate text and visual observations and the demands of each problem. Evaluated across 20 spatial reasoning benchmarks spanning a broad range of static and dynamic 3D/4D spatial reasoning tasks, SpatialClaw achieves 59.9% average accuracy, outperforming the recent spatial agent by +11.2 points, with consistent gains across six VLM backbones from two model families without any benchmark- or model-specific adaptation.
sourceItems.author: Seokju Cho, Ryo Hachiuma, Abhishek Badki, Hang Su, Byung-Kwan Lee, Chan Hee Song, Sifei Liu, Subhashree Radhakrishnan, Seungryong Kim, Yu-Chiang Frank Wang, Min-Hung Chen

trendEntities.title: Automated reproducibility assessments in the social and behavioral sciences using large language models
normalizedKeys: title:automated reproducibility assessments in the social and behavioral sciences using large language models

items.title: Automated reproducibility assessments in the social and behavioral sciences using large language models
items.description: Reproducibility in the social and behavioral sciences is typically evaluated by independent researchers who reanalyze the original data to assess whether the published findings can be recovered. However, such approaches are resource-intensive and difficult to scale. Here, we show that large language models (LLMs) can automate reproducibility assessments. Using N=76 published studies with predefined claims from the behavioral and social sciences, we compare LLM-generated analysis with the origina
items.summary: Reproducibility in the social and behavioral sciences is typically evaluated by independent researchers who reanalyze the original data to assess whether the published findings can be recovered. However, such approaches are resource-intensive and difficult to scale. Here, we show that large language models (LLMs) can automate reproducibility assessments. Using N=76 published studies with predefined claims from the behavioral and social sciences, we compare LLM-generated analysis with the original findings and human reanalysis. For 7 studies, the LLM could not produce a viable effect size estimate. For the remaining studies, our LLM pipeline recovered the original effect sizes in 41% of studies using a +/-0.05 tolerance in Cohen's d. Further, our LLM pipeline reached the same qualitative conclusion as the original study in 96% of cases, where conclusions indicate whether the reanalysis supports the original claim. For comparison, human reanalysts recovered the original effect sizes in 34% of studies and reached the same qualitative conclusion in 74% of cases. Together, these results show that LLMs can serve as a scalable tool for automated reproducibility assessment and provide a foundation for systematic auditing of empirical results in the social and behavioral sciences.
items.author: Tobias Holtdirk, Pietro Marcolongo, Anna Steinberg Schulten, Felix Henninger, Stefan Rose, Sarah Ball, Bolei Ma, Frauke Kreuter, Markus Weinmann, Stefan Feuerriegel
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm

trendEntities.title: Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration
normalizedKeys: title:agents k1 towards agent native knowledge orchestration

items.title: Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration
items.description: Current LLM-based research agents have advanced through agent orchestration, yet largely overlook scientific knowledge orchestration. Existing works often reduce papers to abstracts, surface mentions, and flat \texttt{cites} edges, omitting key entities, claims, evidence, mechanisms, and method lineages essential for scientific reasoning. To this end, we introduce \textbf{Agents-K1}, an end-to-end knowledge orchestration pipeline that converts raw documents into agent-native scientific knowledge
items.summary: Current LLM-based research agents have advanced through agent orchestration, yet largely overlook scientific knowledge orchestration. Existing works often reduce papers to abstracts, surface mentions, and flat \texttt{cites} edges, omitting key entities, claims, evidence, mechanisms, and method lineages essential for scientific reasoning. To this end, we introduce \textbf{Agents-K1}, an end-to-end knowledge orchestration pipeline that converts raw documents into agent-native scientific knowledge graphs. Agents-K1 integrates three components under a unifying theoretical foundation: a multimodal parser whose five-module schema captures entities, multimodal evidence, citations, and typed inter-entity relations across the full paper rather than abstracts alone; a 4B information-extraction backbone trained with GRPO under a rule-based reward; and a graphanything CLI, a tri-source agent interface that unifies web search, multimodal graph retrieval, and cross-document traversal. On top of this, we process 2.46 million scientific papers across six subjects to produce \textbf{Scholar-KG}, of which we release a one-million-paper subset, and the full Scholar-KG is accessible via the SCP link below. The same pipeline can be extended to general-domain corpora and to schema-conformant data synthesis. Extensive experiments demonstrate that Agents-K1 achieves superior performance in scientific information extraction, knowledge graph construction, and multi-hop scientific reasoning.
items.author: Zongsheng Cao, Bihao Zhan, Jinxin Shi, Jiong Wang, Fangchen Yu, Zhijie Zhong, Zijie Guo, Tianshuo Peng, Zhuo Liu, Yi Xie, Xiang Zhuang, Yue Fan, Runmin Ma, Shiyang Feng, Xiangchao Yan, Anran Liu, Peng Ye, Wenlong Zhang, Shufei Zhang, Chunfeng Song, Fenghua Ling, Jie Zhou, Liang He, Bo Zhang, Lei Bai

items.title: Influcoder: Distilling Decoders' Gradient Influence Rankings into an Encoder for Data Attribution
items.description: With the growth of LLMs' (Large Language Models) capabilities, there has been an increasing push to curate high quality datasets by filtering samples in the training data. In general, Data Attribution (DA) methods aim to estimate how individual samples in a training dataset can precondition a model to generate certain outputs. As an example, one might be interested in which samples in the data could be the source of toxic behavior after training the LLM. Many methods quantify this conditioning t
items.summary: With the growth of LLMs' (Large Language Models) capabilities, there has been an increasing push to curate high quality datasets by filtering samples in the training data. In general, Data Attribution (DA) methods aim to estimate how individual samples in a training dataset can precondition a model to generate certain outputs. As an example, one might be interested in which samples in the data could be the source of toxic behavior after training the LLM. Many methods quantify this conditioning through the paradigm of influence functions. While methods of this family are effective in its function, they lack the necessary processing speed and storage compactness to be practically implemented on large datasets. We propose a method, Influcoder, as a quick and cost-effective approach to influence-based Data Attribution at scale.
items.author: Dimitri Kachler, Damien Sileo, Pascal Denis
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, rag

trendEntities.title: HyperTool: Beyond Step-Wise Tool Calls for Tool-Augmented Agents
normalizedKeys: title:hypertool beyond step wise tool calls for tool augmented agents

items.title: HyperTool: Beyond Step-Wise Tool Calls for Tool-Augmented Agents
items.description: Tool-augmented LLM agents commonly rely on step-wise atomic tool calls, where each invocation, observation, and value transfer is exposed in the main reasoning trace. This creates an \emph{execution-granularity mismatch}: locally deterministic tool workflows are unfolded into repeated model-visible decisions, consuming context and forcing the model to manage low-level dataflow in the trace. We introduce \textbf{HyperTool}, a unified executable MCP-style tool interface that changes the model-visi
items.summary: Tool-augmented LLM agents commonly rely on step-wise atomic tool calls, where each invocation, observation, and value transfer is exposed in the main reasoning trace. This creates an \emph{execution-granularity mismatch}: locally deterministic tool workflows are unfolded into repeated model-visible decisions, consuming context and forcing the model to manage low-level dataflow in the trace. We introduce \textbf{HyperTool}, a unified executable MCP-style tool interface that changes the model-visible unit of tool execution. A model invokes HyperTool with a code block that can call existing tools through their original schemas, manipulate returned values, and pass intermediate results locally, folding deterministic tool subroutines into a single outer call. To train models to use this interface, we synthesize HyperTool-format trajectories from cross-tool compositional tasks and verify them in real MCP environments. On MCP-Universe, HyperTool improves average accuracy from 15.69\% to 35.29\% on Qwen3-32B and from 9.93\% to 33.33\% on Qwen3-8B, and surpass GPT-OSS and Kimi-k2.5 on average accuracy, showing that our HyperTool can substantially improve multi-step tool use.
items.author: Yaxin Du, Yifan Zhou, Yujie Ge, Jiajun Wang, Xianghe Pang, Shuo Tang, Tuney Zheng, Bryan Dai, Jian Yang, Siheng Chen
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: llm, agent, rag, tool use, mcp
